title: Task Association and Display
summary: Allow users to enter, manage, and display a single key task for their timer session.
businessValue: As a user, I want to record my primary focus for each session so that I stay committed to my main objective during each Pomodoro.

stories.title: Task Input Field UI Implementation
stories.businessValue: As a user, I want a clear task input field so that I can enter my key focus for the session.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Display task input field on app launch
acceptanceCriteria.given: the user has opened the Pomodoro app
acceptanceCriteria.then: the task input field is visible with placeholder text 'What are you focusing on?'

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Input field styling matches design spec
acceptanceCriteria.given: the task input field is rendered
acceptanceCriteria.then: the input has dark theme styling with cyan focus border per UX spec

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: User can type in task field
acceptanceCriteria.given: the task input field is visible
acceptanceCriteria.when: the user clicks and types in the field
acceptanceCriteria.then: the text is captured and displayed in the input field

definitionOfDone: Task input field renders with correct styling per UX spec
Placeholder text displays correctly
Input accepts and displays user text
Focus state styling applies correctly with cyan border
Component is responsive and centered properly
No accessibility issues detected
Code reviewed and approved
No console errors

stories.title: Task Storage and Persistence Backend
stories.businessValue: As a developer, I want to store tasks per timer session so that user input is retained during the session.

acceptanceCriteria.given: the user enters a task and starts a timer
acceptanceCriteria.when: the CreateTimerSession endpoint is called with task data
acceptanceCriteria.then: the task is stored in the session and persisted to data store

acceptanceCriteria.given: a timer session exists with a task
acceptanceCriteria.then: the response includes the task associated with that session

acceptanceCriteria.given: a task input is provided to create or update a session
acceptanceCriteria.then: the task passes validation and is non-empty with reasonable length limits

acceptanceCriteria.given: a timer session exists with a task
acceptanceCriteria.when: the UpdateTimerSession endpoint is called with new task data
acceptanceCriteria.then: the task is updated in the session and persisted

definitionOfDone: TimerSession data model includes Task property
CreateTimerSession endpoint accepts and stores task data
GetTimerSession endpoint returns task data in response
UpdateTimerSession endpoint updates task data
Task validation logic is implemented and testable
Data is persisted to database or storage layer
API responses follow consistent format
Unit tests cover task storage scenarios
Integration tests cover end-to-end persistence
Code reviewed and approved

stories.title: Task Display During Active Session
stories.businessValue: As a user, I want to see my task while the timer is running so that I stay focused on my primary objective.

acceptanceCriteria.given: the user has entered a task and started the timer
acceptanceCriteria.then: the task is displayed in the key task section below the timer

acceptanceCriteria.given: the user is in focus mode with a task displayed
acceptanceCriteria.when: the user switches to break mode
acceptanceCriteria.then: switching back to focus mode restores the task display

acceptanceCriteria.given: the timer is running with a task displayed
the timer has been reset with a task in the session
acceptanceCriteria.when: the user views the task section

definitionOfDone: Task displays in key task section when timer is active
Task remains visible when paused or reset
Task persists across mode switches
Task styling matches design spec (20px, centered)
Task text is readable with proper contrast
Task section layout is responsive
Component renders without errors
Tested in focus and break modes
Code reviewed and approved

stories.title: Task Editing Before Timer Starts
stories.businessValue: As a user, I want to edit my task before starting the timer so that I can correct or refine my focus objective.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Edit task when timer is stopped
acceptanceCriteria.given: the timer is in stopped state
acceptanceCriteria.when: the user modifies the text in the task input field
acceptanceCriteria.then: the input accepts the new text without restriction

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Task input disabled during timer run
acceptanceCriteria.then: the task input field is disabled to prevent accidental changes

acceptanceCriteria.given: the user has paused the timer
acceptanceCriteria.when: the user views the task input
acceptanceCriteria.then: the input is enabled and can be edited

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Task changes are sent to backend
acceptanceCriteria.given: the user edits the task while timer is stopped
acceptanceCriteria.then: the UpdateTimerSession endpoint is called with the new task

definitionOfDone: Task input is enabled when timer is stopped
Task input is disabled when timer is running
Task input is re-enabled when timer is paused
Disabled state styling is visually distinct
Changes are captured and sent to backend
User cannot accidentally modify task during active focus
Tested with start, pause, and reset scenarios
Code reviewed and approved

stories.title: Task Clearing Functionality
stories.businessValue: As a user, I want to clear my task so that I can start a new session with a fresh focus objective.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Clear button visible when timer stopped
acceptanceCriteria.when: the user views the key task section
acceptanceCriteria.then: a clear button is visible near the task input with red styling

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Clear button hidden when timer running
acceptanceCriteria.when: the user views the key task section
acceptanceCriteria.then: the clear button is hidden or disabled

acceptanceCriteria.given: a task is entered in the input field
acceptanceCriteria.when: the user clicks the clear button
acceptanceCriteria.then: the task input is cleared and becomes empty

acceptanceCriteria.given: a timer session has a task
acceptanceCriteria.then: the UpdateTimerSession endpoint is called to clear the task

definitionOfDone: Clear button renders when timer is stopped
Clear button is hidden or disabled when timer is running
Clear button removes text from input field
Backend endpoint clears task from session
Button uses error color (#F87171) per design spec
Button shows proper hover state
Clear action sends update to backend
Tested in various timer states
Code reviewed and approved
